published.title: Gemini 3.5 Flash vs 3.1 Flash Lite: When 'Flash' Stopped Meaning Cheap
published.angle: Gemini 3.5 Flash launched at $1.50/$9.00 per 1M tokens — a 3× price hike over the prior Gemini 3 Flash — carrying a 6× output multiplier that matches the priciest flagship tiers, yet Google markets it as 'Flash'; meanwhile Gemini 3.1 Flash Lite at $0.25/$1.50 is now the only true budget Gemini route, and Anthropic quietly slashed Claude Opus 4.8 Fast Mode to $10/$50 (down from $30/$150 on Opus 4.7 Fast), proving that 'Flash' and 'Fast' tier labels require a price-table check, not a brand assumption.

published.title: Agentic Coding Model Prices Compared: Grok Build 0.1 vs Claude Opus 4.8 Fast
published.angle: Two purpose-built agentic coding models launched within days of each other — Grok Build 0.1 at $1/$2 per 1M tokens and Claude Opus 4.8 Fast at $10/$50 — and they share an identical 2× output multiplier, yet sit at a 10× price gap; the non-obvious insight is that Anthropic quietly slashed its Fast-mode premium from a punishing 6× speed surcharge (Opus 4.7 Fast at $30/$150) to a rational 2× (Opus 4.8 Fast at $10/$50), meaning the real story isn't Grok vs Claude but how the Fast-tier pricing war is compressing the cost of speed itself.

published.title: Opus 4.8 vs GPT-5.5 vs Gemini 3.5 Flash: Frontier Price Parity Broken
published.angle: For the first time at the frontier tier, sticker prices have nominally converged — Claude Opus 4.8 and GPT-5.5 both land at $5/$25 per 1M tokens — yet the rate cards diverge sharply once you layer in Fast Mode (Anthropic's $10/$50, now 3× cheaper than Opus 4.7 Fast), output multipliers (GPT-5.5 carries a punishing 6× vs Anthropic's 5×), and Gemini 3.5 Flash's $1.50/$9.00 which, despite being marketed as 'Flash', now costs 6× more than Gemini 3.1 Flash Lite and carries the same 6× output multiplier as the flagship GPT-5.5 Pro at $30/$180.
